title: Non-zero exit code with podman exec after the container stops
body:
Issue: podman exec exits with non-zero exit code after the container stops, even though the container's main process exited 0.

Expected Outcome: podman exec exits with the same exit code the container's main process does, after it stops.

Example: Running a container for 10 sec, exec /bin/bash inside it and check exit code after the container stops.

```
podman run -it debian:bookworm sleep 10

podman exec -itl /bin/bash; echo $?

137
```
Only solution I've found is to locate the exec process inside the container and send `SIGTERM`.

But there has to be a better way, right?

comments.body:
That's because the exec process is a separate process so it really doesn't matter how the main process exits, using the same exit code would just be a straight up lie.

What happens is that once the main pid exits all processes in the pid namespace get killed by the kernel so this is not something we can influence even. If you need this you need to build your own graceful exit system by stopping all the exec process first before the main process dies.
